(7) Learning Hexo from Scratch - How to Install Plugins
Time to install some plugins for our Hexo project.

Hexo's basic features — writing, dev server, etc. — are honestly more than enough on their own. But in real use, you'll want some plugins along for the ride. Things like deploying your blog to a web host, or adding search functionality — those rely on plugins.
in an earlier post. If you tried running it, you probably noticed nothing happened:
That's because we haven't installed the required plugin yet, so no matter what you type, nothing happens. I'll cover what
needs and how to configure it in a later post.
For now let's install our first plugin — RSS. Hexo doesn't ship with RSS support, so when you click the RSS button at the top of your blog, you'll find it doesn't work:
To get RSS working you need an extra plugin. Open the VSCode terminal and run:
plugin to generate the RSS, and the feed gets auto-compiled.
After it installs, fire up the dev server again and click the RSS button. The page should open and display properly this time.
One more thing — you might be tempted to install a search plugin. But when you spin up the dev server and try searching, you'll notice it's still going to Google. That's because the default
theme doesn't support local search (i.e. searching within your own blog). Local search has to be supported by the theme itself.
That's the Hexo intro for today. See you tomorrow
